verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN51) encodes a type I integral membrane protein that cycles constitutively between the trans-Golgi network and early/recycling endosomes, with the overwhelming steady-state pool residing in the TGN. Its lumenal/extracellular domain faces the Golgi lumen and endosomal interior — both topologically equivalent to extracellular space — but is never stably present at the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Checking every contextual bucket: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress/activation-induced PM surface display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-specific PM expression reported; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — cycles to early endosomes, not lysosomes, and no lysosomal exocytosis to PM documented; (4) dual_localization — transient PM passage during recycling is a minor intermediate, not a stable PM pool; surface biotinylation/flow studies on intact cells show negligible steady-state PM signal; (5) stable_surface_attachment — not a secreted protein. No therapeutic programs target it on the cell surface. The protein is an endomembrane resident by function and localization.
